challenges.instruction: Learn these Somali linking chunks, then answer mixed prompts one at a time.

rows.label: oo
rows.prompt: Maxamed and his wife, who is called Khadra
answers: Maxamed iyo xaaskiisa oo Khadra la yidhaahdo.

rows.label: si uu u
rows.prompt: so that he may begin his work

lessonSlides.title: These chunks glue ideas together
lessonSlides.description: Somali often uses short linking pieces that do a specific job inside a larger sentence. Study the chunk and the full phrase together.
focusPoints: Use `inuu` for a clause like `that he ...`

rows.label: oo
rows.prompt: Maxamed and his wife, who is called Khadra
answers: Maxamed iyo xaaskiisa oo Khadra la yidhaahdo.

lessonSlides.title: Purpose and movement need different connectors
lessonSlides.description: Some chunks show purpose, while others combine with a subject marker to show movement from somewhere. In practice, the cue will change but the connector still does the same job.
focusPoints: Use `si uu u` for `so that he may ...`
Use `ayuu ka` in a full sentence for `he ... from ...`
Focus on what the chunk does in the sentence

rows.label: si uu u
rows.prompt: so that he may begin his work

practiceRows.label: oo
practiceRows.prompt: the woman who is called Caasha
practiceRows.rule: Use `oo` to add descriptive information to the noun phrase.

practiceRows.label: inuu
practiceRows.rule: Use `inuu` for a clause meaning `that he ...`.

practiceRows.label: ka
practiceRows.rule: Use `ka` for `from`, with `baan` or `ayaan` for `I` in a full sentence.

practiceRows.label: si uu u
practiceRows.prompt: so that he may learn Somali
practiceRows.rule: Use `si uu u` for `so that he may ...`.

practiceRows.label: uu + ka
practiceRows.rule: Use `ayuu ka` in a full sentence for `he ... from ...`.
